title: AesKey::cache vs CmacKey::cache: inconsistent length guard
description: AesKey::cache (aes.rs:22) uses if !matches!(key_bytes.len(), 16 | 24 | 32) { return Err(...) } while CmacKey::cache (cmac.rs:22) uses match key_bytes.len() { 16 | 24 | 32 => {} _ => return Err(...) } for exactly the same validation. They should use the same idiom — prefer if !matches!(...) for the guard form, since it reads as a guard rather than a dispatch. This inconsistency is a distraction when reading both files back-to-back.
